Programme Aim

To provide all students with the knowledge and skills required to deliver a person-centred approach to health and social care within a multi-agency, multidisciplinary setting — regardless of current employment status.

The programme develops the skills required within the changing landscape of health and social care, enabling students to support the most vulnerable in society and make a real difference to the communities they serve.

Evidence-informed practice is at the heart of everything we do, with a proactive approach to addressing real practice-related issues.

🤝 Collaborative

Working across agencies and disciplines to deliver joined-up care.

🧭 Person-Centred

Putting the individual at the heart of every decision.

🔬 Evidence-Based

Using research and best practice to drive real outcomes.

BSc (Hons) CHSCOffer Holder Day 2026

Programme Objectives

🎯 Graduate Skills

Promote the development of graduate and employability skills that enable you to demonstrate confidence and readiness for the working environment.

🤝 Collaborative Working

Create opportunities to study and engage with issues that encourage collaborative working to deliver safe, effective, person-centred, compassionate care.

🌍 Flexible Delivery

Facilitate flexible and multi-campus delivery in line with the UWS Corporate Strategy and Curriculum Framework.

This degree provides excellent opportunities to learn with people from a variety of backgrounds and interests within a community of professionals and academics at UWS.
BSc (Hons) CHSCOffer Holder Day 2026

Programme Themes

Three themes run throughout the programme, building your knowledge and skills progressively across each year.

📚

Academic, Professional & Personal Development

Building your confidence, skills and professional identity from day one through the ASPIRE modules.

🏥

Contemporary Health & Social Care

Understanding the complex, evolving landscape of integrated health and social care services.

🔍

Research Skill Development

Learning to find, evaluate and apply evidence to improve practice and outcomes.

BSc (Hons) CHSCOffer Holder Day 2026

Entry Requirements

Level 7 — Year 1

  • GCSE: grades A, B or C
  • Irish Leaving Cert: Ordinary level (C2+)
  • A-Level: grades B, C, C
  • BTEC 3
  • SQA National Qualifications / Edexcel Foundation

Level 8 — Year 2

  • HNC / BTEC 4 in any subject area
  • International equivalents reviewed for equivalency
  • HNC of 96 credits = advanced standing
Many students join at Level 8 with an HNC from college.

Level 9 — Year 3

  • HND / BTEC 5 / DipHE in an appropriate health or social care subject

Not sure if your qualifications fit? We're happy to discuss your individual circumstances.

BSc (Hons) CHSCOffer Holder Day 2026
Year 1 — Level 7 Modules
⏱️ ASPIRE Core
💬 Communication for Health & Social Care Core
🏥 Foundations of Health & Social Care Core
🤝 First Steps: Collaborative Health/Social Core
🔬 Foundations in Evidence Based Practice Core
❤️ Promoting Health & Wellbeing Core
Year 1 sets the context for your student journey — you'll develop core academic, personal and professional skills and be introduced to the key concepts in health & social care.
BSc (Hons) CHSCOffer Holder Day 2026
Year 2 — Level 8 Modules
⏱️ ASPIRE 2 Core
🔍 Evidence Based Practice & Research Core
🤝 Next Steps: Collaborative H&SC Core
🧠 Empowering Personhood Core
💭 Psychology of Health & Wellbeing Core
⚖️ Dimensions of Health & Social Equality Core
Deepening your knowledge — In Year 2 you'll revisit key topics in more depth: research approaches, person-centred care, empowering service users, public health and psychology.
BSc (Hons) CHSCOffer Holder Day 2026
Year 3 — Level 9 Modules
Term 1
📊 Critical Research Appraisal Core

Plus 2 of the following 3:

🌐 Contemporary Issues in H&SC Option
📈 Quality Improvement in H&SC Option
🕊️ Supporting People Facing Loss Option
Term 2
💬 Therapeutic Communication Core

Plus 2 of the following 3:

🕯️ Introduction to Palliative Care Option
🔗 Context of Integration Option
🧠 Living Well with Dementia Option
Your choice matters — Option modules let you tailor your studies to the area of health & social care you're most interested in.
BSc (Hons) CHSCOffer Holder Day 2026
Year 4 — Level 10 (Honours)
Core Module
📝 Dissertation (Double Module)

Your dissertation is in a health/social care subject of your choosing — a capstone project that leads you toward employment in the area you aspire to work in.

Option Modules (Choose 4)

Term 1

👥 Leadership in H&SC
👨‍👩‍👧 Supporting Families

Term 2

🌍 Improving Population Health
📖 Effective Teaching in Practice
Year 4 develops you as a professional — You'll gain skills to lead a team, teach others in your workplace, and develop quality improvement approaches.

Plus the option of one Year 3 module in Term 1 or Term 2.

A Typical Week at UWS

🏫

On Campus

Tutorials, workshops, and group activities with your lecturers and fellow students.

💻

Online via Aula (VLE)

Asynchronous learning materials, discussions, and activities you complete in your own time.

📖

Independent Study

Reading, research, and working on your assessments.

3
20-credit modules per term
12–15
hours contact time per week
Hybrid learning approach — A combination of on-campus and online sessions designed to be flexible.

Assessments include: essays, case studies, presentations, reports and examinations.

BSc (Hons) CHSCOffer Holder Day 2026

The Six C's of Care

These six values form the foundation of health and social care practice and are embedded throughout the programme.

❤️ Care

Care is our core business — it defines us and our work.

💛 Compassion

How care is given through relationships based on empathy, respect and dignity.

⭐ Competence

Having the expertise, clinical knowledge and skills to deliver effective care.

💬 Communication

Central to successful caring relationships and effective team working.

🦁 Courage

The courage to do the right thing, to speak up and embrace new ways of working.

🤝 Commitment

A commitment to patients and the public — the cornerstone of what we do.

BSc (Hons) CHSCOffer Holder Day 2026

What is Person-Centred Care?

Person-centred care means treating individuals with dignity, compassion and respect. It involves working in partnership with patients, families and communities to understand their needs and preferences.

This approach is central to modern health and social care practice and is a key theme throughout the CHSC programme.

Watch this short video to explore what person-centred care looks like in real practice:
youtube.com/watch?v=pj-AvTOdk2Q

Person-centred care made simple

What everyone should know about person-centred care

The Health Foundation

BSc (Hons) CHSCOffer Holder Day 2026

Evidence Based Practice

Understanding and using evidence is a core skill developed throughout the programme. Evidence comes in many forms:

👤 Witnesses

Individuals with firsthand knowledge of an event or situation.

📄 Documents

Written records such as contracts, letters, emails and official reports.

💾 Digital Data

Information stored electronically — computer files, text messages, social media.

🧪 Expert Testimony

Opinions and analyses from individuals with specialised knowledge.

📸 Photos & Videos

Visual recordings that capture events or conditions.

🔬 Scientific Reports

Findings from scientific studies or experiments.

📊 Official Statistics

Data from reputable organisations like national statistical agencies.

📚 Peer-Reviewed Journals

Articles evaluated by field experts (e.g. Nature, The Lancet, Science).

BSc (Hons) CHSCOffer Holder Day 2026

Sources of Robust Evidence

📖

Peer-Reviewed Journals

Evaluated by experts for accuracy and quality.

🏛️

Government & Official Reports

Published by agencies like WHO — generally reliable.

📚

Academic Books

Written by experts, published by university presses.

🔬

Empirical Studies

Based on verifiable observations and replicable data.

📊

Systematic Reviews & Meta-Analyses

Synthesise results from multiple studies for comprehensive overview.

🧑‍⚕️

Expert Testimony

Specialist opinions, especially when corroborated by other evidence.

📈

Official Statistics

Data from reputable national and international organisations.

Student Support at UWS

🏠 The Student Hub

Your first point of contact on every campus. Available in person, by phone, WhatsApp, email and online.

hub@uws.ac.uk · 0141 848 3800

💚 Counselling & Wellbeing

Free, confidential support for all UWS students. A safe, affirming environment for whatever you're going through.

🎯 Careers Service

Support during your studies and for up to 5 years after graduation. 1-to-1 advice, workshops and employer networking.

📝 Academic Skills Team

Help with study skills, academic writing, research methods and presentation skills.

🤝 Student Success Team

Proactive support to keep you on track, plus Student Buddies — experienced students who've been through it all.

🆘 24/7 Support

Health Assured helpline for confidential support any time, day or night.

0800 028 3766

Also: Disability Service · Funding & Advice · Multifaith Chaplaincy · SafeZone App · International Student Support
